title: Intersections
description:
You are given the following two functions. ($\mathbb{N}_0$ denotes the set of non-negative integers.)

$$f(x)= \begin{cases} 0, & \mbox{if } x < 0 \\ x - 2k, & \mbox{if } 2k \le x < 2k+1 \, (k \in \mathbb{N}_0) \\ -x + 2(k+1) & \mbox{if } 2k + 1 \le x < 2k+2 \, (k \in \mathbb{N}_0) \end{cases}$$

$$g(x) = {x \over a}$$

How many intersections between the graphs of $y=f(x)$ and $y=g(x)$ exist on the XY-plane?
input: The first and only line of the input contains a single integer $a$.
output: Print the number of intersections between the graphs of $y = f(x)$ and $y = g(x)$. If there are infinitely many intersections, print INF.
source: eric00513, chansol, dj035, jenny00513, lms0806, MenOfPassion, mjhmjh1104, parkky, tony9402
